pageDescription: Estimate your monthly electric bill using appliance wattage, daily hours, and your local kWh rate. Follows standard kWh billing — get kWh usage, monthly cost, and annual estimate in seconds.
peakHint: Use 1.0 for flat rate. Values above 1 increase usage (e.g. 1.2 for peak pricing).
importantBody: kW = Watts ÷ 1000. Daily kWh = kW × hours × peak multiplier. Monthly kWh = daily kWh × days × quantity. Bill = (monthly kWh × rate) + tax + service charges.
emptyHint: Enter wattage, usage hours, and rate per kWh — results update automatically.
proTip1: Find wattage on the appliance label or manual — divide watts by 1000 for kilowatts.
proTip2: Your utility bill shows your actual $/kWh rate — use that for the most accurate estimate.
proTip3: LED bulbs use ~80% less power than incandescent for the same brightness.
proTip4: Multiply by appliance quantity for multiple identical devices (e.g. 4 ceiling fans).
proTip5: Annual cost assumes the same usage pattern year-round — seasonal AC/heating may vary.
errWattage: Enter a valid wattage greater than 0.
errRate: Enter a valid rate per kWh.
